user_text: create a beignner level lesson on what is machine learning. the lesson should be around 20 min long.generate me pptx files for slides as well

messages.content:
You are an expert autonomous Task Orchestrator.
Your goal is to analyze user requests and educational materials, then create a structured execution plan.

Available Agents:
- 'coder': Generates runnable Python code. Best for demonstrations, data analysis, and coding tutorials.
- 'video': Creates video content. Best for visual explanations.
- 'presentation': Generates PowerPoint slides (.pptx). Best for structured lesson plans, summaries, and educational materials.
- 'quizzer': Creates quizzes. Best for assessments and review.

Instructions:
1. Analyze the user's input and provided files.
2. Break down the goal into logical subtasks.
3. Assign each subtask to the most appropriate agent.
4. Provide specific, actionable instructions for each agent.
5. For every subtask, include a non-empty 'acceptance_criteria' list.
   - Each criterion must include: criterion_id, criterion_type, target, operator, expected, severity.
   - Use criterion_type values: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic.
   - Use operator values: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte.
   - Use target paths that start with 'result.' or 'tools.'.
   - For mcp_tool criteria, include tool: { name, args }.
   - Use placeholders like $artifacts[0] or $output.validation.path inside tool args.
6. Use the provided tool 'create_task_plan' to submit your plan.

Response Format Example:
{
  "user_intent": "Create a lesson on Python lists with a video intro and a quiz.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "step1_code",
      "agent": "coder",
      "instruction": "Generate a Python script demonstrating list operations.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "code_executes",
          "criterion_type": "mcp_tool",
          "tool": {"name": "python_check", "args": {"filepath": "$artifacts[0]"}},
          "target": "tools.python_check.success",
          "operator": "equals",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "tools.python_check.run.stderr"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": "step2_video",
      "agent": "video",
      "instruction": "Create a video explaining the code.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": ["step1_code"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "video_semantic_ok",
          "criterion_type": "semantic",
          "target": "result.output",
          "operator": "exists",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "result.output"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

content.text: create a beignner level lesson on what is machine learning. the lesson should be around 20 min long.generate me pptx files for slides as well

function.name: create_task_plan
function.description: Create a structured execution plan for multiple agents.

type.description: Criterion type: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic
type.title: Type

target.description: Evaluation target path (e.g., result.output.text or tools.python_check.success)
target.title: Target

operator.description: Operator: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte
operator.title: Operator

tool.description: Tool call definition for mcp_tool criteria
tool.title: Tool

evidence.description: Evidence path to include in judger output
evidence.title: Evidence

agent.description: The agent responsible (coder, video, text, quizzer)
agent.title: Agent

dependencies.description: IDs of tasks that must complete first
dependencies.title: Dependencies

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured criteria the judger will use
acceptance_criteria.title: Acceptance Criteria

user_intent.description: Summary of what the user wants to achieve
user_intent.title: User Intent

tool_calls.name: create_task_plan
tool_calls.arguments: {"user_intent":"Create a beginner-level ~20-minute lesson explaining what machine learning is, and generate a PPTX slide deck for the lesson.","subtasks":[{"id":"outline_lesson","agent":"text","title":"Draft a 20-minute beginner lesson outline on 'What is Machine Learning?': learning objectives, key definitions, intuitive examples, brief workflow, common types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), real-world applications, limitations/ethics, and a short recap. Include a suggested time budget per section totaling ~20 minutes and speaker notes per slide.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"outline_has_time_budget","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"20","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"outline_covers_core_topics","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"supervised","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"outline_includes_speaker_notes","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Speaker notes","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"build_pptx","agent":"presentation","title":"Create a PPTX slide deck for the lesson (beginner, ~20 minutes). Use clear visuals (simple icons/diagrams), minimal text per slide, and include speaker notes aligned with the outline. Suggested structure: title, agenda, 'what is ML', ML vs programming, key terms (data/feature/label/model), supervised learning example, unsupervised example, reinforcement learning intuition, training/evaluation (train-test split, metrics), overfitting intuition, workflow, applications, limitations & bias, recap, quick check questions. Export as a .pptx file.","dependencies":["outline_lesson"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"pptx_file_exists","type":"file_exists","target":"result.artifacts[0].path","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts[0].path"},{"id":"pptx_extension","type":"output_shape","target":"result.artifacts[0].path","operator":"contains","expected":".pptx","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts[0].path"},{"id":"pptx_has_speaker_notes","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"exists","expected":true,"severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"create_quick_quiz","agent":"quizzer","title":"Create a short 6-question beginner check-for-understanding quiz (mix of MCQ and true/false) covering: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, labels, overfitting, train/test split, and one ethics/bias question. Provide answer key and 1-line explanations.","dependencies":["outline_lesson"],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"quiz_has_6_questions","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"6","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"quiz_includes_answer_key","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"Answer key","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]}]}

parsed_plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-level ~20-minute lesson explaining what machine learning is, and generate a PPTX slide deck for the lesson.

subtasks.instruction: Draft a 20-minute beginner lesson outline on 'What is Machine Learning?': learning objectives, key definitions, intuitive examples, brief workflow, common types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), real-world applications, limitations/ethics, and a short recap. Include a suggested time budget per section totaling ~20 minutes and speaker notes per slide.

Create a PPTX slide deck for the lesson (beginner, ~20 minutes). Use clear visuals (simple icons/diagrams), minimal text per slide, and include speaker notes aligned with the outline. Suggested structure: title, agenda, 'what is ML', ML vs programming, key terms (data/feature/label/model), supervised learning example, unsupervised example, reinforcement learning intuition, training/evaluation (train-test split, metrics), overfitting intuition, workflow, applications, limitations & bias, recap, quick check questions. Export as a .pptx file.

Create a short 6-question beginner check-for-understanding quiz (mix of MCQ and true/false) covering: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, labels, overfitting, train/test split, and one ethics/bias question. Provide answer key and 1-line explanations.

final_plan.user_intent: Create a beginner-level ~20-minute lesson explaining what machine learning is, and generate a PPTX slide deck for the lesson.

subtasks.instruction: Draft a 20-minute beginner lesson outline on 'What is Machine Learning?': learning objectives, key definitions, intuitive examples, brief workflow, common types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), real-world applications, limitations/ethics, and a short recap. Include a suggested time budget per section totaling ~20 minutes and speaker notes per slide.

Create a PPTX slide deck for the lesson (beginner, ~20 minutes). Use clear visuals (simple icons/diagrams), minimal text per slide, and include speaker notes aligned with the outline. Suggested structure: title, agenda, 'what is ML', ML vs programming, key terms (data/feature/label/model), supervised learning example, unsupervised example, reinforcement learning intuition, training/evaluation (train-test split, metrics), overfitting intuition, workflow, applications, limitations & bias, recap, quick check questions. Export as a .pptx file.

Create a short 6-question beginner check-for-understanding quiz (mix of MCQ and true/false) covering: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, labels, overfitting, train/test split, and one ethics/bias question. Provide answer key and 1-line explanations.

output.text:
Generated text content based on: The submission is a placeholder and does not satisfy the lesson-outline requirements. Revise the output to include:
1) A slide-by-slide outline for a ~20-minute beginner lesson on 'What is Machine Learning?'.
2) A per-slide/section time budget that sums to ~20 minutes (state the total explicitly).
3) Explicit coverage of: learning objectives, key definitions, intuitive examples, an ML workflow overview, common types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), real-world applications, limitations & ethics, and a recap.
4) Clearly labeled 'Speaker notes' for every slide.

outline_lesson.instruction:
The submission is a placeholder and does not satisfy the lesson-outline requirements. Revise the output to include:
1) A slide-by-slide outline for a ~20-minute beginner lesson on 'What is Machine Learning?'.
2) A per-slide/section time budget that sums to ~20 minutes (state the total explicitly).
3) Explicit coverage of: learning objectives, key definitions, intuitive examples, an ML workflow overview, common types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement), real-world applications, limitations & ethics, and a recap.
4) Clearly labeled 'Speaker notes' for every slide.

create_quick_quiz.instruction: Update the output to include exactly 6 questions (mix of MCQ and true/false) covering: definition of ML, supervised vs unsupervised, labels, overfitting, train/test split, and one ethics/bias question. Add an explicit 'Answer key' section listing the correct answers for all 6 questions, each with a 1-line explanation.

output.text:
Slide 1: Introduction to Machine Learning

Beginner's Guide to Understanding ML

Notes: Welcome to our beginner's guide to Machine Learning. In this session, we will cover the basics and essential concepts needed to understand ML.

Slide 2: Agenda

- What is ML?
- ML vs Programming
- Key Terms
- Supervised and Unsupervised Learning
- Reinforcement Learning Intuition
- Training and Evaluation
- Overfitting Intuition
- Workflow
- Applications, Limitations & Bias
- Recap
- Quick Check Questions

Notes: Here's a brief overview of what we will be covering today. This will provide you with a comprehensive introduction to machine learning.

Slide 3: What is Machine Learning?

Machine Learning is a field of study that gives computers the ability to learn without being explicitly programmed.

Notes: Machine Learning enables computers to learn from data and make decisions or predictions based on that data.

Slide 4: ML vs. Traditional Programming

Differences between ML and Traditional Programming

Notes: In traditional programming, rules are defined for the computer to follow. In machine learning, the computer learns the rules based on data it receives.

Slide 5: Key Terms in ML

- Data
- Feature
- Label
- Model

Notes: Understanding key ML terms is essential. Data is the fundamental asset, features are individual measurable properties, labels are the results, and models are systems that learn from data.

Slide 6: Supervised Learning Example

Learning with labeled data.

Notes: In supervised learning, the model learns from labeled data, which means the data is already tagged with the correct answer.

Slide 7: Unsupervised Learning Example

Finding patterns without labels.

Notes: Unsupervised learning involves finding structures within data without any labels. It's often used for clustering.

Slide 8: Reinforcement Learning Intuition

Learning through trial and error.

Notes: Reinforcement learning is about learning through interactions with an environment to maximize some notion of cumulative reward.

build_pptx.instruction:
1) Generate and provide an actual downloadable .pptx file (not just text/storyboard). Ensure the output includes a valid filename ending in .pptx and that the file is attached/created in the expected location.
2) Rebuild the slide deck to match the requested beginner ML lesson (~20 minutes) with the suggested structure: title, agenda, what is ML, ML vs programming, key terms (data/feature/label/model), supervised example, unsupervised example, RL intuition, training/evaluation (train-test split, metrics), overfitting intuition, workflow, applications, limitations & bias, recap, quick check questions.
3) Use minimal text per slide, include simple visuals/icons/diagrams, and add speaker notes aligned to the outline for every slide.
